author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
Examination of the Sixth Charge and Answers.
title: Examination of the Sixth Charge and Answers.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: Well, now, had the judges either the power or the right to condemn these unrecognised and vague words, when no person on the other side was present to assert that Pelagius had written the very culpable sentences which were alleged to have been addressed by him to the widow?

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: In such a matter, it surely could not be enough to produce a manuscript, and to read out of it words as his, if there were not also witnesses forthcoming in case he denied, on the words being read out, that they ever dropped from his pen.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: But even here the judges did all that lay in their power to do, when they asked Pelagius whether he would anathematize the persons who held such sentiments as he declared he had never himself propounded either in speech or in writing.

verses.book: A Work on the Proceedings of Pelagius.
verses.text: And when he answered that he did anathematize them as fools, what right had the judges to push the inquiry any further on the matter, in the absence of Pelagius’ opponents?
